# DO NOT EDIT BY HAND. This file is generated/locked from pyproject.toml.
#
# pyproject.toml is the source of truth for dependencies (with version
# constraints). This file should be a fully pinned + HASHED lockfile so CI and
# the Docker build install a reproducible, verified dependency set.
#
# SCOPE: these are the APPLICATION runtime deps only (LangChain + the
# Gemini/Vertex provider + LangGraph core). The self-hosted Agent Server runtime
# (langgraph-api), its Postgres/Redis drivers, and the server-managed durable
# checkpointer are provided by the `langchain/langgraph-api` base image (see
# agent/Dockerfile) — they are intentionally NOT listed here and must not be
# pip-installed by hand (doing so conflicts with the server-managed persistence).
#
# Regenerate after changing pyproject.toml dependencies:
#
#   # Recommended (fast, writes uv.lock; export a pip-installable, hashed file):
#   uv lock
#   uv export --format requirements-txt --no-emit-project --no-dev \
#       --no-hashes --output-file requirements.txt
#   # (add --generate-hashes / drop --no-hashes once you want --require-hashes)
#
#   # OR, without committing to uv.lock:
#   uv pip compile pyproject.toml --generate-hashes -o requirements.txt
#
# Install in CI / Docker:   pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.txt
#
# NOTE: the exact pins + hashes are intentionally NOT fabricated here. Run one
# of the commands above on a clean checkout to populate them. Until then, the
# unpinned lines below mirror pyproject's runtime deps so the project still
# installs (without hash verification).

langchain>=1.0,<2.0
langchain-google-genai>=4.2,<5.0
langgraph>=1.0,<2.0
deepagents>=0.6,<0.7
langchain-tavily>=0.2,<0.3
